text.title: Preview + apply pairs for destructive operations
text.summary: Split destructive or expensive operations into two tools so the agent reasons over the diff before committing the side effect.

section.title: The pair pattern
section.p: `*_preview` is a pure function: takes the operation parameters, returns the would-be result (diff, cost estimate, affected IDs) WITHOUT side effects. `*_apply` accepts the preview's hash plus an idempotency key and performs the action only if the hash still matches the current world state.

rule.statement: Operations that delete data, charge money, deploy infrastructure, or send irreversible communications MUST be exposed as a `*_preview` (no side effect) + `*_apply` (idempotent, requires preview hash) pair. The single-call form MUST NOT exist in the catalog.
rule.rationale: Splits the model's plan step from the commit step. The agent — or a guard agent or human — can inspect the preview, reject obvious mistakes (deleting prod, refunding wrong customer), and only then call `*_apply`. The hash makes the apply phase a TOCTOU-safe commit: if the world changed between preview and apply, the apply fails fast.

example.note: Agent can show the diff to the user before applying. Re-running apply with the same key is a no-op replay; if `main` moved, the hash mismatches and apply rejects with `STALE_PREVIEW`.
Model picks the tool, deploy runs, no chance to inspect, no idempotency. A retry on timeout double-deploys.

antipattern.description: `*_preview` that internally calls the upstream "dry-run" but still mutates a cache or counter.
antipattern.reason: If preview has any observable side effect, agents cannot safely run it speculatively, and the safety property collapses.

section.reference: DevX — Scalable AI Agents: 10 Design Patterns.
